sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.8 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: Let$G$be the free product of free groups$A$and$B$, and$V$the verbal subgroup of~$G$corresponding to the equation$x^4 = 1$. Is it true that, if$a A V$and$b B V$, then$(ab)^2 V$? { V.\,G.\,Vilyatser} No, it is not. If$A$and$B$are free groups with bases$a_1,a_2$and$b_1,b_2$, respectively, then, for example, the commutators$a=[[a_1,a_2,a_2],[a_1,a_2]]$and$b=[[b_1,b_2,b_2],[b_1,b_2]]$do not belong to$V...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
